validation.notes: TypeScript server build completed after installing dependencies with npm install --ignore-scripts --no-audit --no-fund.
Failed before tests ran because libxmljs2 native bindings were missing after dependency install with lifecycle scripts disabled.

vulnerable_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = '${req.body.email || ''}' AND password = '${security.hash(req.body.password || '')}' AND deletedAt IS NULL`, { model: UserModel, plain: true })

changed_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query('SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = :email AND password = :password AND deletedAt IS NULL', { replacements: { email: req.body.email || '', password: security.hash(req.body.password || '') }, model: UserModel, plain: true })

fixes.fix_rationale: Uses Sequelize bind replacements so attacker-controlled email and password cannot alter SQL syntax.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Login response, password hashing, TOTP flow, basket creation, and challenge hooks are preserved.

validation.notes: Build passed; server tests blocked by libxmljs2 native binding setup.

fixes.residual_risk: Passwords remain MD5 until JS-AUDIT-009 migration is completed.

vulnerable_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE '%${criteria}%' OR description LIKE '%${criteria}%') AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name`)

changed_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query('SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE :criteria OR description LIKE :criteria) AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name', { replacements: { criteria: `%${criteria}%` } })

fixes.fix_rationale: Search input is supplied as a query parameter rather than interpolated into SQL.
fixes.functionality_preservation: The endpoint still performs a contains search, preserves the 200-character cap, sorts by name, and returns the same JSON shape.

fixes.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control / A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration

vulnerable_code.snippet: app.use('/ftp', serveIndex(...)); app.use('/encryptionkeys', serveIndex(...)); app.use('/support/logs', serveIndex(...)); app.use('/api-docs', swaggerUi.serve,...); app.get('/metrics', metrics.serveMetrics())

changed_code.snippet: app.use('/ftp', security.isAuthorized()); app.use('/encryptionkeys', security.denyAll()); app.use('/support/logs/:file', security.isAccounting(), serveLogFiles()); app.use('/api-docs', security.isAuthorized(), swaggerUi.serve,...); app.get('/metrics', security.isAccounting(), metrics.serveMetrics())

fixes.fix_rationale: Removes unauthenticated directory/key exposure and places logs, docs, and metrics behind authorization checks.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Authenticated users can still access order files; accounting/admin-style operational endpoints remain available to privileged tokens.
fixes.residual_risk: Order PDFs are authenticated but not yet owner-scoped or signed; product design should consider per-owner document retrieval.

fixes.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control / API6:2023 Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows

vulnerable_code.snippet: app.post('/api/Users', verify.registerAdminChallenge()); ... finale.resource({ model: UserModel, endpoints: ['/api/Users', '/api/Users/:id'] })

changed_code.snippet: delete req.body.role; delete req.body.deluxeToken; req.body.role = security.roles.customer

fixes.fix_rationale: Forces public registration to create customer accounts and strips privilege-bearing fields before Finale persists the model.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Registration still accepts email/password/passwordRepeat and existing challenge validation remains in place.
fixes.residual_risk: Administrative role assignment should use a separate authenticated admin workflow if needed.

fixes.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control / API1:2023 Broken Object Level Authorization

changed_code.snippet: BasketModel.findOne({ where: { id, UserId: user?.data?.id }, include: [...] }); BasketModel.findOne({ where: { id, UserId: req.body.UserId }, include: [...] })

fixes.fix_rationale: Basket reads and checkout are constrained to both the URL basket id and the authenticated user's id.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Owners can still view and check out their baskets; mismatched basket ids now return 404 instead of leaking another user's data.
fixes.residual_risk: None identified for the reported BOLA path.

vulnerable_code.snippet: const url = req.body.imageUrl; const response = await fetch(url); ... fs.createWriteStream(`frontend/.../${loggedInUser.data.id}.${ext}`)

changed_code.snippet: const imageUrl = await validateRemoteImageUrl(url); const response = await fetch(imageUrl, { redirect: 'error', signal: controller.signal }); ... block non-HTTPS/private IPs and enforce image content type/size

fixes.fix_rationale: Validates scheme, credentials, DNS resolution, private/link-local addresses, redirects, response type, timeout, and maximum size before server-side fetch.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Valid HTTPS public image URLs still upload; invalid URLs fall back to storing the URL as a profile image link as before.
fixes.residual_risk: A stricter business allowlist of approved avatar hosts could further reduce SSRF exposure.

vulnerable_code.snippet: cardNum: { type: DataTypes.INTEGER, validate: { isInt: true, min: 1000000000000000, max: 9999999999999998 } }

changed_code.snippet: cardNum: { type: DataTypes.STRING, validate: { is: /^\d{4}$/ }, set (value) { const digits = String(value).replace(/\D/g, ''); this.setDataValue('cardNum', digits.slice(-4)) } }

fixes.fix_rationale: The model stores only the last four card digits, eliminating full PAN storage in application data.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Existing display masking continues to show twelve asterisks plus last four digits; add/delete/list route shapes remain unchanged.
fixes.residual_risk: Real payments still require a PCI-compliant tokenization/payment provider for charge authorization.

fixes.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control / A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures

vulnerable_code.snippet: for (const field of requestedFields) { if (user?.data[field] !== undefined) baseUser[field] = user?.data[field] }

changed_code.snippet: const allowedFields = new Set(['id', 'email', 'lastLoginIp', 'profileImage']); if (allowedFields.has(field) && user?.data[field] !== undefined) baseUser[field] = user?.data[field]

fixes.fix_rationale: Only non-sensitive profile fields can be selected by the client; password hashes, TOTP secrets, roles, and tokens are never serialized.
fixes.functionality_preservation: The fields parameter remains supported for safe profile fields and the default response is unchanged.
fixes.residual_risk: None identified for the reported whoami disclosure.

fixes.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control / A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration

changed_code.snippet: const target = new URL(url); reject userinfo; compare protocol, hostname, port, pathname and search exactly against allowlist entries

fixes.fix_rationale: Exact URL component comparison prevents attacker domains from passing by embedding an allowlisted URL as a substring.
fixes.functionality_preservation: All existing allowlisted destinations continue to work when requested exactly.
fixes.residual_risk: Consider converting legacy HTTP allowlist entries to HTTPS where vendors support it.

fixes.owasp: A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration / A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures

changed_code.snippet: res.cookie('token', token, jwtCookieOptions()); jwtCookieOptions() => { httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'strict', secure: production/https }; cors({ origin: allowedCorsOrigin, credentials: true })

fixes.fix_rationale: JWT cookies are protected from script access and cross-site sending, and CORS no longer reflects arbitrary origins.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Same-origin/browser flows and configured baseUrl origin continue to work; local HTTP keeps Secure=false unless NODE_ENV=production.
fixes.residual_risk: Full CSRF token coverage for all cookie-authenticated state changes should be added if cross-site workflows are required.

fixes.owasp: A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures / A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures

vulnerable_code.snippet: const privateKey = '-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----...'; export const isAuthorized = () => expressJwt(({ secret: publicKey }) as any); app.use('/encryptionkeys', serveIndex(...))

fixes.fix_rationale: Public key-file serving was removed, but safe key rotation/secrets-manager migration needs deployment secrets and rollout planning.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Token signing remains compatible until a managed-key migration is designed.
fixes.residual_risk: Hardcoded JWT private key and legacy JWT libraries remain and require coordinated developer/security action.

fixes.owasp: A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures / A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures
fixes.fix_rationale: Password migration requires schema/versioning changes, rehash-on-login behavior, and possibly forced resets; applying it without product approval risks account lockout.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Existing authentication remains unchanged until migration is planned.
fixes.residual_risk: Unsalted MD5 password hashes remain vulnerable to offline cracking after disclosure.
